channel.title: News & Events on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science
channel.description: Recent content in News & Events on IQSS Clone - Harvard Institute for Quantitative Social Science

item.title: Forgotten Graves: A New Lens on Colonial Slavery
item.description: In the heart of Cambridge, Jason Ur, Stephen Phillips Professor of Archaeology and Ethnology and IQSS affiliate, is unearthing histories long obscured.

Do you have news to share?

We’re always interested in hearing the latest work and accomplishments from IQSS affiliates and would be happy to help you share your news. If you have any updates, let us know!

item.title: A focus on flourishing: Newest IQSS program combines social science, philosophy to understand the factors that lead to a good life

item.title: The art of the online interface
item.description:
New desktop application streamlines connection between IQSS’s Dataverse and Sid

February 02, 2026

Join the Beta Test

Interested in contributing to the LOOP app development?

drafts

Contact Len Wisniewski

Sid is a version of Open OnDemand developed at IQSS with the goal to unify and enhance the research computing user experience at Harvard.

item.title: News
item.description:
News

Search Within Results

Search Within Results

search

335 results

Forgotten Graves: A New Lens on Colonial Slavery

April 03, 2026

When the COVID-19 Pandemic kept him from continuing his research abroad, faculty affiliate Jason Ur made an archaeological discovery in Harvard’s own neighborhood.

item.title: Events
item.description:
Events

Watch our calendar for year-round opportunities for training, consulting, and learning at IQSS.

Research Technology Training

IQSS offers technical training in the areas listed below. Several are available only during the academic year, so be sure to check back frequently.DataverseGISPythonRRCEStataUNIXSee research technology training events
